message:
fix: inject fake k8s clients in TestClusterGroupsCRUD for CI (#3770)

* fix: warn users about non-interactive terminal in agent missions (#3767)

When an AI mission runs install/deploy commands, the agent terminal does
not support stdin. This caused hangs when commands required interactive
input (e.g., GitHub Copilot CLI needing `gh auth login`).

Changes:
- Add non-interactive instruction to the default system prompt so all
  AI providers avoid running commands that need user input
- Inject an explicit non-interactive reminder into deploy/install mission
  prompts sent to the agent
- Show a system message in mission chat warning users that interactive
  input is not supported
- Improve Copilot CLI error detection to surface auth-related failures
  with actionable guidance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: inject fake k8s clients in TestClusterGroupsCRUD for CI (#3760)

The test used clusters "c1" and "c2" without injecting fake dynamic
clients, causing LabelClusterNodes to fail with "no such file or
directory" for ~/.kube/config in CI. The handler then returned 207
(Multi-Status) instead of the expected 201. Fix by injecting fake
dynamic clients with the nodes GVR for both clusters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@ import (
 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/resource"
 	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
 	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime"
+	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema"
 	k8sfake "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake"
 	k8sscheme "k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme"
 	"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd/api"
@@ -290,6 +291,13 @@ func TestClusterGroupsCRUD(t *testing.T) {
 	env := setupTestEnv(t)
 	handler := NewWorkloadHandlers(env.K8sClient, env.Hub, env.Store)
 
+	// Inject fake dynamic clients for clusters used in the test so that
+	// LabelClusterNodes / RemoveClusterNodeLabels can resolve them without
+	// a real kubeconfig (fixes CI where ~/.kube/config doesn't exist).
+	nodeGVR := schema.GroupVersionResource{Version: "v1", Resource: "nodes"}
+	injectDynamicCluster(env, "c1", map[schema.GroupVersionResource]string{nodeGVR: "NodeList"})
+	injectDynamicCluster(env, "c2", map[schema.GroupVersionResource]string{nodeGVR: "NodeList"})
+
 	env.App.Get("/api/cluster-groups", handler.ListClusterGroups)
 	env.App.Post("/api/cluster-groups", handler.CreateClusterGroup)
 	env.App.Put("/api/cluster-groups/:name", handler.UpdateClusterGroup)
